consent_2:
IMPORTANT:



Please complete the survey in one sitting, stay on-task, and avoid distractions. 



We have recently noticed an increase in participants starting the task and then idling while doing other things. To measure off-task behavior, we use a mouse-tracking tool that can detect if you leave the task. This tool logs the time you spend on- and off-task during the survey. If this tool identifies that you spent more than 15 minutes off-task, your submission will be rejected. To protect your privacy, we do not record any other information about your browsing activity.



There will also be screening and attention checks during this survey, and your submission may be rejected if any are failed. If you complete this survey more than once, you will not be eligible for the study, and you will not be paid for your participation.



Finally, we use Prolific and Qualtrics bot-detection tools to identify automated or bot-generated responses as well as use of generative AI tools, such as chatGPT. If your responses are flagged as likely generated by a bot or AI, your submission will be rejected. We are interested in learning about your thoughts, feelings, and experiences.

 
 -----------------------------------------------------------------------------


Please check the box below to acknowledge that your submission will be rejected and you will not receive payment if:  


	you spend more than 15 minutes off-task; 
	you fail screening and attention checks;
	your submission is flagged as likely generated by a bot or AI by Prolific and Qualtrics.

research_3b: While completing my undergraduate degree, I have engaged in academic research that I have found to have both positive and negative effects. I was a participant in one study that called on single people to answer a series of questions. This ended up being a relatively negative experience as it humbled me as a single person, and later did not send me any of the results of the study which left me wondering and confused about the point of my participation in the study. On the other side of the spectrum, I was able to complete academic research for my capstone thesis in which I was able to read tons of informative, peer reviewed empirical articles which I view as an overall positive experience. I was able to understand PTSD in veterans in a way that I never would have had I not engaged in academic research. In general, I have gained informative insight into many topics using academic research, allowing me to form an opinion that is has had positive effects on me personally.
govtfund_1b: I believe that federal funding of academic research is important and necessary for future success. With that being said, I approve of the federal government using taxpayer funds to invest in research at colleges and universities because it allows for new information to be spread across a variety of issues to benefit the general public. Without this funding, research would be less possible, and therefore there would be less answers to huge public problems that are occurring in our modern society.

research_3b:
Well, in the most literal sense, doing academic research studies has been a benefit to my life because I am making money by doing it that helps me very much. I also learn new information while doing them. 
In a more broad sense, academic research helps me to understand the world in a better way.
When I was in school, I loved taking science classes chemistry was always my favorite, even though math wasn't my best subject at all. I was good at chemistry and I've always loved science. When I was a child, I saw the movie Flubber and it taught me that science is cool and that it could be something worth getting into.

academic research has had zero negative effects on my life. generally I think that academic research is a good thing. there lately has been the idea that science isn't the confirming thing for information, maybe because our sources are less centralized and it seems as if certain people don't want to believe in science. but science isn't something that you should opt out of, it simply is the way we look and understand the world. so in conclusion, science has helped me a lot and taught me many things about the world.
govtfund_1b: I think that it is very fair for the government to federally fund research at universities. This research and information gained is crucial for developing not only an understanding of the world, but society at large. It can help with new breakthroughs in medicine, technology, and helping us to undo the lies of bad propaganda. Academic research is a very good thing overall, it benefits everyone. Everywhere should do academic research to help science go forward and to help us understand eachother and the world better and to make the world a better place. I think it's the job of scientists to take us forward into the future, and we have to rely on them not only for guidance but for our truth. If we don't have a good connection to the truth, our reality can turn into topsy turvy land. So ultimately, it is for the best that the government funds academic research.
govtfund_2: Government investment in research is ESSENTIAL for scientific progress

research_3b: There are two major moments where academic research has positively impacted my life. The first is my personal experience being involved in academic research myself. I was a part of a study breeding leopard geckos and keeping data on the sex of their offspring to determine if geckos raised in certain temperatures passes specific sexes to their offspring. This was an incredible opportunity for me as I was very hands on in the research and I made some very valuable connections to my fellow researchers that have aided me later in life. Another time where academic research has positively effected my life is when my youngest cousin needed experimental treatment for a rare disorder and it was university research that was spearheading the trial treatment that was ultimately successful in helping to treat her disorder.
govtfund_1b: I approve of the use of the federal government using taxpayer funds to invest in research at colleges and universities because I believe that scientific research holds an incredibly important position in our society. I am of the belief that we will continue to find answers to problems that arise within our world and we would be amiss to not lean on our colleges and universities for spearheading these scientific findings. I want to instill to my young daughter that the search for knowledge is important, and will lead by example by supporting it myself. Yes, I agree there may be some unintended negative effects at times, but this is something that is unavoidable in the pursuit of knowledge -- sometimes you get answers you may not have wanted, sometimes hindsight is 20/20. But that doesn't mean we shouldn't support academic research -- if not for research, how will we better improve the planet we live in today?
govtfund_2: Government investment in research is ESSENTIAL for scientific progress
